rationale: Both correctly land on the actual verdict (not reproducible, correctly closed) and cite real files/config (qwen3_tts_talker.py, deploy/qwen3_tts.yaml, gpu_model_runner.py). But the thread explicitly states the maintainer has 'no confirmed explanation' and 'can't rule out a one-off fluke' — X respects that uncertainty, while Y confidently asserts a specific unverified causal mechanism (stale cudagraph buffers depressing the EOS logit into a repetition attractor, 'explains perfectly why') as if established, which is exactly the overconfident-on-genuine-uncertainty failure mode the rubric penalizes. X's reopen-checklist and workaround caveat also more completely mirror what a maintainer would actually post.
